client_context.current_process: Small team manually answers repetitive FAQs during 9am–6pm ET
client_context.goal: Automate first layer of customer support via AI chatbot
client_context.market: US consumer-facing, must respect US data norms and communication style

explicit_capabilities.title: Answer FAQs using existing knowledge base / help docs
explicit_capabilities.gradable: Demo retrieves and cites FAQ answers from a knowledge base

explicit_capabilities.title: Pull basic order status info
explicit_capabilities.gradable: Demo shows a simulated order lookup (mock API or static data) that bot can query

explicit_capabilities.title: Smooth handoff to human agent when bot can't resolve
explicit_capabilities.gradable: Demo includes escalation flow; routes to working hours (9am–6pm ET); ticket confirmation shown

explicit_capabilities.title: Embeddable on website; connects to help desk (Zendesk or open to alternatives)
explicit_capabilities.gradable: Demo is embeddable iFrame; Zendesk integration design shown in proposal deck

explicit_capabilities.title: US data-handling norms and US consumer communication style
explicit_capabilities.gradable: No international jargon; respectful US retail tone; no data leakage concerns in demo flow

proposal_deliverables.title: Architecture explanation + rationale
proposal_deliverables.gradable: Proposal (cover letter + deck) explains in-browser RAG design and why it suits this use case

proposal_deliverables.title: Examples of similar chatbots/agents with links (portfolio or illustrative)
proposal_deliverables.gradable: Proposal includes 1–2 reference projects; honest about what's built vs illustrative

proposal_deliverables.title: Rough timeline and price breakdown
proposal_deliverables.gradable: Cover letter or deck states estimated delivery time and cost structure

proposal_deliverables.title: Recommendation: fixed price or hourly for this project
proposal_deliverables.gradable: Proposal explicitly advises fixed-price or hourly model with reasoning

proposal_deliverables.title: Explicit confirmation: 'Michael is currently based in the United States'
proposal_deliverables.gradable: Cover letter or deck states this clearly

hard_constraints.description: AI must run FREE with ZERO paid API tokens. Use in-browser LLM (WebLLM/WebGPU with Llama-3.2-1B/3B or Phi-3.5-mini) or Transformers.js WASM fallback.
hard_constraints.rationale: Client wants no recurring fees; in-browser is the selling point.

hard_constraints.description: RAG (knowledge base retrieval) must also run client-side. Embed FAQ corpus as JSON asset or use Transformers.js for embeddings.
hard_constraints.rationale: Demonstrates 'no external dependencies, easy to maintain' story.

hard_constraints.description: Order status lookup uses mock data (static JSON queried client-side). No real backend integration needed.
hard_constraints.rationale: Keeps demo fully self-contained and honest about scope.

hard_constraints.description: Handoff and escalation are simulated in-demo (form + confirmation). Real Zendesk API call out of scope for demo; integration design shown in deck.
hard_constraints.rationale: Demonstrates the flow without requiring client credentials.

design_direction.aesthetic: Modern SaaS support-tool UI — clean, trustworthy, professional. Suited to US consumer support (not michaelwegter.com's dark gallery-wall theme).
design_direction.palette: TBD by researcher/planner; should evoke trust and efficiency (consider soft blues, grays, accent green for positive actions).
design_direction.tone: Friendly but professional; US retail customer service voice (no formal jargon, clear and direct).
design_direction.avoid: michaelwegter.com's dark theme, mustard/cyan palette, Space Grotesk; em-dashes or en-dashes anywhere

hero_feature.title: In-browser RAG support chatbot
hero_feature.scope: User asks a question → bot retrieves relevant FAQ chunks from knowledge base (client-side) → grounds answer in retrieved sources → cites sources → streams response. Handles order lookups via mock API. Gracefully escalates to human when uncertain.
hero_feature.technical_approach: WebLLM (Llama-3.2-1B/3B-Instruct) via WebGPU if available; Transformers.js WASM as fallback; client-side vector retrieval (Transformers.js embeddings or precomputed JSON index); mock order data as JSON asset.
hero_feature.browser_fallback: WebGPU detection → if absent, try Transformers.js; if still unsupported, RAG-only template responder (no generation, just retrieval + templates). Always show honest 'running locally' status + model-download progress.

supporting_features.title: Human handoff + escalation with ET business-hours routing
supporting_features.scope: Escalation form; checks current time in ET; routes to queue if outside 9am–6pm; ticket confirmation screen. Simulates Zendesk integration (no real API).

supporting_features.title: Admin panel: Knowledge-base editor + chat analytics
supporting_features.scope: Shows maintainability. Admin can add/edit FAQ entries (stored in browser localStorage or demo-config JSON). Analytics dashboard shows chat deflection metrics, top questions, sources cited. Demonstrates the product is easy to own after handoff.

demo_deliverables.form: Self-contained static app (index.html + assets/, no build step OR small Vite app with base: '/demos/<slug>/'). CDN: WebLLM, Transformers.js. No Flask blueprint needed unless a mock backend strengthens the story.
demo_deliverables.routes: Main chat interface, admin panel toggle, settings (model choice, debug mode).
demo_deliverables.required_assets: FAQ corpus (JSON), mock orders (JSON), model weights (CDN-hosted or bundled), demo design CSS (bespoke, not portfolio).

proposal_deliverables_detailed.cover_letter: Addresses client's 5 capabilities, proposes in-browser architecture, links to live demo, includes timeline, price, and US-based confirmation.
proposal_deliverables_detailed.one_pager: Visual summary of the chatbot (screenshots from demo), architecture diagram, feature checklist, business value (no recurring token cost, privacy, easy to maintain), demo link.
proposal_deliverables_detailed.pptx_deck: Title, problem → solution flow, architecture + tech stack, demo screenshots, Zendesk integration design (how it connects), timeline, pricing, team/credentials, CTA. All styled to match the support-tool design language, NOT the portfolio.

test_plan.deploy_test: Playwright on live site (Chromium supports WebGPU flags). Test: chat with grounded FAQ answer, order lookup, escalation form, admin panel. Account for model-download latency and headless WebGPU support.
acceptance_criteria: Demo loads with no console errors
Chat responds with grounded FAQ answers and cited sources
Escalation form appears, honors ET hours, shows ticket confirmation
Admin panel loads and allows KB edits
WebGPU fallback works if WebGPU unavailable
All 5 proposal must-includes present in deliverables
Design is bespoke to support tool, no portfolio styling
No em/en dashes in any text

token_discipline.reuse_pattern: Clone michaelwegter.com's app chrome (src/data/apps.js, Apps.jsx, AppFrame.jsx) for site integration. Write demo logic (chat, RAG, escalation) fresh.
token_discipline.self_contained: Ship FAQ + orders as JSON assets in /demos/<slug>/assets/. No backend calls (except optional mock blueprint for 'API integration' storytelling).
token_discipline.pass_paths: Between steps, reference files by path, not full contents. Read handoffs first; open full files only when detail is needed.
